zh.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: 英國藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Baynes於1521年出生在英國越可爾洛維爾。她於1542年11月19日與Richard Moore結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1623年在英國享年102歲，埋葬在英國越可爾丹特。

nameMeanings.name: Baynes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish (of Norman origin): variant of   Baines  .

variants.name: Baines
meanings: English (northern) and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the places in France called Bains, in either Ille-et-Vilaine or Maine-et-Loire, from Old French baine ‘bath’.
Scottish and northern English: variant of Bain or Bane , with excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Baynes was born in 1521, in Rothwell, Yorkshire, England. She married Richard Moore on 19 November 1542. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1623, in England, at the age of 102, and was buried in Dent, Yorkshire, England.

fr.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Baynes est née en 1521, à Rothwell, Everwyck, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Richard Moore le 19 novembre 1542. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1623, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 102 ans, et a été enterrée à Dent, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Baynesは1521年にイギリスヨークシュアイアロスウエルで生まれた。1542年11月19日にRichard Mooreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1623年にイギリスで102歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアデントにて埋葬された。

ko.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: 영국 랭카실르 뱅크 홀 니아어 플레스턴
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Baynes(은)는 1521년 영국 요르크실르 로스웰에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1542년 11월 19일 Richard Moore(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1623년 영국에서 102세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 덴트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Baynes nasceu em 1521, em Rothwell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Richard Moore em 19 de novembro de 1542. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1623, em Inglaterra, com 102 anos, e foi sepultada em Dent, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: банк холл нир престон, ланкашир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Baynes родилась в 1521 г. в местности ротвелл, еркшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Moore 19 ноября 1542 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1623 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 102 лет и была похоронена в местности дент, еркшир, Англия.
